# Test apx catalog resolve — resource type -> serving module
#
# Verifies that:
#  1. `apx catalog generate` indexes google.api.resource types per module
#     (resource_types appears in catalog.yaml, no manual entry)
#  2. `apx catalog resolve <type>` returns the serving module coordinates
#  3. an unknown type fails loud
#  4. an ambiguous type (two modules claiming it) fails loud, listing claimants

# Setup: canonical repo with git history
mkdir canonical
cd canonical
exec git init
exec git config user.email 'test@test.com'
exec git config user.name 'Test'
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=apis --skip-git --non-interactive

# Place annotated protos at the module directories (matching the tag identity)
mkdir proto/iam/user/v1
cp $WORK/user.proto proto/iam/user/v1/user.proto
mkdir proto/billing/invoice/v1
cp $WORK/invoice.proto proto/billing/invoice/v1/invoice.proto
# Two modules that both declare the same type -> ambiguous
mkdir proto/dup/a/v1
cp $WORK/dup_a.proto proto/dup/a/v1/dup.proto
mkdir proto/dup/b/v1
cp $WORK/dup_b.proto proto/dup/b/v1/dup.proto

exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init'

# Release tags so the modules land in the catalog
exec git tag proto/iam/user/v1/v1.2.3
exec git tag proto/billing/invoice/v1/v1.0.0
exec git tag proto/dup/a/v1/v1.0.0
exec git tag proto/dup/b/v1/v1.0.0

# Generate: types are indexed from the annotations
exec apx catalog generate --org=testorg --repo=apis
exists catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'resource_types:' catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'iam.example.com/User' catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'billing.example.com/Invoice' catalog/catalog.yaml

# Resolve a known type -> serving module coordinates
exec apx catalog resolve --catalog catalog/catalog.yaml iam.example.com/User
stdout 'proto/iam/user/v1'
stdout 'v1.2.3'

# Resolve as JSON
exec apx --json catalog resolve --catalog catalog/catalog.yaml billing.example.com/Invoice
stdout '"module_id": "proto/billing/invoice/v1"'

# Unknown type fails loud
! exec apx catalog resolve --catalog catalog/catalog.yaml iam.example.com/Ghost
stderr 'iam.example.com/Ghost'

# Ambiguous type fails loud, listing claimants
! exec apx catalog resolve --catalog catalog/catalog.yaml dup.example.com/Thing
stderr 'proto/dup/a/v1'
stderr 'proto/dup/b/v1'

-- user.proto --
syntax = "proto3";
package iam.user.v1;

message User {
  option (google.api.resource) = {
    type: "iam.example.com/User"
    pattern: "users/{user}"
  };
  string name = 1;
}

-- invoice.proto --
syntax = "proto3";
package billing.invoice.v1;

message Invoice {
  option (google.api.resource) = {
    type: "billing.example.com/Invoice"
    pattern: "invoices/{invoice}"
  };
  string id = 1;
}

-- dup_a.proto --
syntax = "proto3";
package dup.a.v1;

message Thing {
  option (google.api.resource) = { type: "dup.example.com/Thing" };
}

-- dup_b.proto --
syntax = "proto3";
package dup.b.v1;

message Thing {
  option (google.api.resource) = { type: "dup.example.com/Thing" };
}
